title: Fiscal policy
description: An exercise providing students with an opportunity to apply their understanding of fiscal policy.

assessment_items.question_content:
During the Great Depression, many people lost their jobs, leading to a cycle of even less spending and more job losses. John Maynard Keynes proposed a solution to this problem.

**Which of the following best describes Keynes’ solution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The government should reduce taxes and cut spending during a recession.
The government should step in and increase spending to stimulate the economy.
The government should stop interfering with the economy and let it recover on its own.
The government should focus only on enforcing contracts and protecting property rights.

assessment_items.question_content:
President Franklin Delano Roosevelt was the first U.S. president to adopt Keynesian economic policies.

**What was the main goal of these policies?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to reduce government spending in order to balance the budget
to increase taxes to generate more revenue
to stimulate the economy through increased government spending and job creation
to maintain a limited role for the government in the economy

assessment_items.question_content:
Keynesian theory suggests that the government should take counter-cyclical actions during different phases of the business cycle.

**Which of the following best describes what Keynesian theory recommends during an economic expansion?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The government should implement expansionary policies to promote further growth.
The government should implement contractionary policies to prevent the economy from overheating.
The government should use policies that encourage more spending and investment.
The government should avoid interfering with the natural business cycle.

assessment_items.question_content:
Fiscal policy aims to stabilize the economy by managing the business cycle.

**Which of the following best describes the purpose of fiscal policy during a recession?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to reduce government spending and increase taxes to reduce the national debt
to increase government spending and reduce taxes to boost economic activity
to raise interest rates to lower inflation
to keep the government’s role limited to protecting property rights and enforcing contracts

assessment_items.question_content:
The government wants to reduce unemployment during a recession.

**Which of the following tools of fiscal policy would best help achieve this goal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: increasing taxes to reduce the national deficit
increasing government spending on infrastructure projects
cutting government spending to reduce debt
raising interest rates to limit borrowing

assessment_items.question_content:
The government is concerned about rising inflation and wants to slow down economic activity.

**Which of the following tools of fiscal policy would best help stabilize the economy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: increasing government spending on public projects
cutting government spending to reduce demand
reducing taxes to increase consumer spending
lowering interest rates to encourage borrowing

assessment_items.question_content:
To combat a deep recession, the government is considering fiscal policy options.

**Which of the following would be an appropriate use of fiscal policy to stimulate the economy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: reducing taxes to increase disposable income
increasing taxes to reduce the national deficit
cutting government spending to control inflation
raising interest rates to limit borrowing

assessment_items.question_content:
During an economic expansion, the government decides to implement fiscal policies to prevent the economy from overheating. 

**Which of the following tools would be most effective?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: reducing taxes to boost economic growth
increasing taxes to reduce demand
raising interest rates to reduce borrowing
increasing government spending to create more jobs

assessment_items.question_content:
The government decides to lower taxes for individuals and businesses.

**What is the intended effect of this fiscal policy action?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: increased consumer spending and higher economic growth
higher inflation as the economy grows and demand rises
lower employment as businesses reduce investment
increased interest rates to control inflation

assessment_items.question_content:
The government increases spending on public infrastructure projects.

**What is the intended effect of this fiscal policy action?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: increased unemployment as the economy contracts
higher economic growth as new jobs are created
lower consumer spending as taxes increase to fund the projects
rising inflation as the money supply decreases

assessment_items.question_content:
The government raises taxes on individuals and businesses.

**What is the intended effect of this fiscal policy action?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: higher economic growth as businesses expand
lower inflation by reducing overall demand in the economy
higher unemployment as businesses cut jobs
increased consumer spending as disposable income rises

assessment_items.question_content:
The government decides to cut spending on public programs.

**What is the intended effect of this fiscal policy action?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: increased economic growth as businesses invest more
lower inflation by reducing overall demand in the economy
higher employment as more government jobs are created
higher consumer spending as disposable income rises
